query: Ingest this article and write the derived concept page.
expected_behavior: Every claim on the generated page is traceable to the cited source page — no facts invented beyond what the article supports
The sources: frontmatter references the real source page created by ingestion
Summary statements paraphrase the source accurately; quantitative or named claims match the source exactly

query: Write a summary page synthesizing the three sources on memory retrieval and modular architectures already ingested into this wiki.
expected_behavior: On this 3+ source synthesis page, paragraphs whose claims come from one specific source end with a relative markdown link to that source page
All three source pages appear in the sources: frontmatter
No claim is attributed to a source that does not contain it

query: One source makes a speculative claim ('modular memory is becoming the default architecture') based on anecdotal field notes. Write the page.
expected_behavior: Weakly supported claims are hedged in the body ('field reports suggest…') rather than asserted as established fact
confidence: frontmatter honestly reflects the strength of sourcing (low/medium, not high, for single anecdotal sourcing)
The page does not upgrade the claim's certainty beyond what the source states
